img = np.ascontiguousarray(img)
return torch.from_numpy(img), labels_out, self.im_files[index], shapes

for i, (imgs, targets, paths, _) in pbar:
    imgs = imgs.to(device, non_blocking=True).float() / 255  # uint8 to float32, 0-255 to 0.0-1.0

inputs.semantic_hint: Batch of images as a float32 tensor of shape (B, 3, H, W), normalized to [0, 1]. Loaded as BGR, converted to RGB and transposed from HWC to CHW in Dataset.__getitem__. Resized/letterboxed to imgsz (e.g. 640). Divided by 255 in training loop.

ground_truths.semantic_hint: Detection targets tensor of shape (N_total, 6) where each row is [image_index, class, x_center, y_center, width, height]. Coordinates are normalized [0,1]. image_index is set by collate_fn to associate targets with images in the batch. Variable number of targets per image; concatenated across the batch.

proposed_mapping.name: unknown
proposed_mapping.notes: Map the image tensor (B, 3, H, W) as the model input encoder. Per-sample shape is (3, imgsz, imgsz), uint8 -> float32 / 255.

proposed_mapping.name: unknown
proposed_mapping.notes: Map the detection targets (variable-length per sample, columns: [image_index, class, cx, cy, w, h]) as the ground-truth encoder. Note: image_index column is batch-relative and set by collate_fn; per-sample labels have shape (num_objects, 5) with columns [class, cx, cy, w, h] before collation.

comments: This is a standard YOLOv5 repository (Ultralytics) adapted for VisDrone dataset. The framework is unambiguously PyTorch. The primary dataset class is LoadImagesAndLabels which yields (image_tensor, labels_tensor, file_path, shapes) per sample. The collate_fn stacks images into (B,3,H,W) and concatenates variable-length labels into (N_total,6) with a prepended batch-image index. The model receives the image batch directly, and ComputeLoss receives both model predictions (multi-scale feature maps) and the targets tensor. Labels follow YOLO format: [class, cx, cy, w, h] normalized to [0,1].
